claim: ElevenLabs Instant Voice Cloning recommends ~1-2 minutes of clean audio; 30-second samples can yield excellent results, while >3 minutes can be detrimental. Professional Voice Cloning wants ~1-3 hours.
quote: Approximately 1-2 minutes of clear audio without any reverb, artifacts, or background noise of any kind is recommended... users have seen samples of only 30 seconds and get excellent results... avoid recording more than 3 minutes.

claim: Modern AI voice tools can generate a convincing voice model from as little as 3 seconds of audio scraped from TikTok, voicemail greetings, YouTube or Instagram, with ~90% accuracy.
quote: low-cost AI tools can clone a human voice with nearly 90% accuracy using a sample of just a few seconds of audio — often scraped from a Facebook video, a TikTok post, or a voicemail

claim: In April 2024 a LastPass employee received WhatsApp calls, texts and a voicemail using a deepfake of CEO Karim Toubba's voice; the employee ignored it and reported it, with zero impact.
quote: one of our employees received a series of calls, texts, and at least one voicemail featuring an audio deepfake from a threat actor impersonating our CEO via WhatsApp

claim: The LastPass deepfake attempt used WhatsApp — outside normal LastPass business channels — which tipped off the employee.
quote: the use of WhatsApp as a communication channel... is also outside of normal business communication channels

claim: The FCC finalized a $6M fine in September 2024 against Steve Kramer for the AI-generated Biden robocall sent to NH primary voters in January 2024; Lingo Telecom paid $1M for distributing it.
quote: FCC Issues $6M Fine For N.H. Robocalls

claim: Kramer was indicted on 13 felony counts of voter suppression and 13 misdemeanor counts of impersonating a candidate in NH; he claimed the deepfake was a 'warning' about AI dangers.
quote: 13 counts of voter suppression, a felony, and 13 counts of impersonating a candidate, a misdemeanor

claim: OpenAI Voice Engine — first previewed in early 2024 with a 15-second sample claim — remained unreleased as of 2025-2026, restricted to 'trusted partners' over abuse fears.
quote: the tool remains in preview roughly a year after its initial announcement, with OpenAI giving no indication as to when it might launch

claim: OpenAI's Voice Engine ships with watermarking, requires speaker consent, and demands clear AI-disclosure to listeners, citing election-year risks.
quote: Safety measures like watermarking and proactive monitoring are in place... developers must obtain 'explicit consent' from the original speaker

claim: ElevenLabs operates a 'No-Go Voices' classifier that blocks creation of clones approximating presidential candidates and other high-risk public figures.
quote: restricts the creation of voice clones that approximate the voices of political figures, including those actively involved in presidential elections in the US and UK

claim: Pindrop Pulse detects synthetic and bot speech in real time from ~2 seconds of audio, claiming up to 99.2% accuracy and <1% false-positive rate when paired with multifactor authentication.
quote: Pindrop detects synthetic and bot-generated speech in real time using just two seconds of audio, with up to 99.2% accuracy

claim: In an NPR-run benchmark, Pindrop Pulse correctly identified 81 of 84 deepfake audio samples (96.4% accuracy), beating other commercial detectors.
quote: Pindrop® Pulse emerged as a clear leader, boasting a 96.4% accuracy rate in identifying AI-generated audio, detecting 81 out of 84 audio samples correctly

claim: McAfee's 7,000-person global survey found 70% of people were not confident they could distinguish a cloned voice from the real one; 1 in 4 had experienced a voice-clone scam or knew someone who had, and 77% of targeted victims lost money.
quote: 70% of people in a worldwide survey said they weren't confident they could tell the difference between a cloned voice and the real thing

claim: A finance worker at engineering firm Arup in Hong Kong was tricked into wiring $25M across 15 transactions in January 2024 after a video conference where deepfakes posed as the UK-based CFO and other colleagues.
quote: A finance worker at a multinational firm was tricked into paying out $25 million to fraudsters using deepfake technology to pose as the company's chief financial officer in a video conference call

claim: In March 2025, a finance director at a Singapore multinational authorized a US$499,000 payment after a Zoom meeting in which the CFO and other executives were entirely AI-generated.
quote: a finance director at a multinational firm in Singapore authorised a US$499,000 payment during what appeared to be a Zoom call with the company's senior leadership. None of the people on the call were real.

claim: Deepfake-enabled vishing surged ~1,600% in Q1 2025 vs late 2024, and dark-web markets sell turnkey vishing-as-a-service from a $1,000 base price with voice-cloning kits and LLM wrappers tuned to bypass filters.
quote: Deepfake-enabled vishing surged by over 1,600% in the first quarter of 2025

claim: The FBI reported $893M in 2025 losses tied to AI-related scams (voice clones for 'family in distress' calls and investment deepfakes), with older adults accounting for $352M; AARP estimates 1-in-5 over-65s targeted, average victim age 74, average loss $18,000.
quote: $893 million in losses tied to AI-related scams, including voice cloning for 'family in distress' calls and deepfakes in investment schemes, with older adults accounting for $352 million

claim: NCC Group researchers built a real-time voice-cloning vishing rig in about an hour using only publicly available short audio clips and off-the-shelf hardware, then successfully extracted sensitive info from real organizations in red-team engagements.
quote: trained an AI voice cloning tool using a publicly available, short audio clip and within about an hour managed to develop a vishing system that could mimic any voice in real-time

claim: Mandiant's M-Trends 2026 reports voice phishing climbed to the second-most-common initial infection vector in 2025, present in 11% of investigations where a vector could be identified.
quote: voice phishing climbed to the second-most common initial infection vector in 2025, appearing in 11% of Mandiant investigations
